title: Sida periptera
taxonomy: Sida periptera is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Malvaceae. It is a part of the genus Sida, which includes various species of plants native to tropical and subtropical regions. The plant belongs to the order Malvales and the class Magnoliopsida. The classification of Sida periptera is further supported by its characteristics and physical attributes, such as its leaves and flowers. As a member of the Malvaceae family, Sida periptera shares similarities with other plants in this family, such as hibiscus and okra.
uses: Sida periptera, also known as the hairy fanpetals or fanpetals, has various medicinal uses. The plant is traditionally used in African and Asian cultures to treat a range of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. The leaves and roots of Sida periptera are used in herbal medicine to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. Additionally, the plant is said to have antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating infections and wounds. Further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness of Sida periptera as a treatment for these conditions.

side_effects: Sida periptera, also known as the horsefly tree, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes, including treating fever and skin conditions. However, its use can lead to some side effects, including skin irritation and allergic reactions in some individuals. intro: Sida periptera, also known as the hairy fanpetals or fanpetal, is a species of flowering plant in the family Malvaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Sida periptera include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal effects.
